mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weak consumer demand, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the rising unemployment signal over a 24-month evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real sales of existing homes and housing starts indicates synchronized strength in the residential demand cycle, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns, with the interaction term coefficient insignificant at the 5% level in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values reflect expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days during periods of stable monetary policy.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases yield negative 20-day returns when FEDFUNDS remains unchanged for 3 consecutive months across multiple rolling test windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the consumer price index exceeding 0.3 percent triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL spikes above the threshold consistently precede positive 5-day market returns over a 24-month evaluation window, breaking the tightening expectation channel.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates confirms robust labor market tightening and wage growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 when tested on out-of-sample data from 2010 to 2013.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects broad-based economic expansion and rising corporate earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward returns across multiple rolling 3-year windows, invalidating the earnings channel.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the industrial production index exceeding 1.0 percent signals a sudden contraction in manufacturing output and supply chain disruptions, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO drops below the threshold but SPX records positive 5-day returns in > 70% of observed cases, refuting the supply shock transmission mechanism.
